\paragraph{Canonical Statement.}
ID3660 introduces Fibonacci Connection Scaling Law as a source-extracted canonical equation candidate. The equation is promoted from the Tier A equation-source intake and remains pending source-context expansion, duplicate review against the earlier TZPID corpus, Wolfram normalization, symbol-key enrichment, and derivation/proof-lane construction.
\paragraph{Canonical Equation.}
\begin{equation} \mathcal{A}_{\text{Fibonacci}} = \Module\left[\begin{aligned} &\text{Geometry}: S^2 \text{ with radius } R = 0.1\text{ m} \\ &\text{Material}: \text{Nd}_2\text{Fe}_{14}\text{B with }\mu_r \sim 10^3 \\ &\text{Engravings}: \{r_i, \theta_i, \phi_i\} = \text{FibonacciLattice}[N = 987] \\ &\text{Field}: |\mathbf{B}| = 14\text{ T (saturation)} \\ &\text{Temperature}: T = 4.2\text{ K (liquid helium)} \end{aligned}\right] \end{equation}
